name: Likeke Falls
tagline: A short jungle scramble to a waterfall that feels way more remote than it actually is.
parking: The Ko'olau Golf Club is permanently closed. Street parking outside the former golf club gate on Kionaole Rd; park completely off the road to avoid tickets. Trail access via the old maintenance road.
bestTime: After rain for the best water flow — morning for fewer people
talkStory: The trail passes through the Ko'olau watershed system that ancient Hawaiians managed through irrigation channels feeding taro lo'i in the valleys below. The 15-ft waterfall drops into a mossy grotto that feels impossibly remote despite being minutes from Pali Highway. Connect this to Old Pali Road for a loop past the site of the 1795 Battle of Nu'uanu, where Kamehameha I drove O'ahu's warriors over the pali cliffs.
